title: The Long Account
description: An elf warlock's reckoning. Caelen Vyr signed his soul to a fiend to win the power the Empire denied his people — and now he spends it pulling elves out of the Reach's cages, one rescue at a time, until the night a White Cape finally notices. A three-chapter arc of mercy curdling into vengeance.
introduction:
They struck your people from the imperial rolls a generation ago — unrostered, unsanctioned, unpersoned. The White Capes called it order. Your family called it a slow death, and they were right.

You did not accept it. In a ruin the Capes had forgotten, you found a voice in the shadows that offered a trade: power now, the account settled later. You signed. The fire that answers your hand is not yours — it is borrowed, at terrible interest — but it pulls elves out of bandit cages and off slavers' stakes, and for now that is enough to keep the bargain feeling like mercy instead of what it is.

For now. The Reach is full of cages, and sooner or later, doing this kind of work in the open, a White Cape is going to look up from his ledger and see you.
aiContext:
THE LONG ACCOUNT is a three-chapter, tonally dark arc for an elf Fiend-pact Warlock — the premade character Caelen Vyr (level 3-4). Premise: the Empire struck the Reach's elves from the imperial rolls; the White Capes (human wizards, the state's magical regulators) enforce that erasure. Caelen made a pact with a fiend for the power to fight back, and uses it to free captive elves. The fiend's price is unstated and looming — every rescue is also a debt.

Arc: (CH1 The Roadside Cage) Caelen frees a few elves from bandit slavers in the wardstone wood — a clean, small mercy that establishes his power and his cause. (CH2 The Season of Passage) He hits a larger holding — the 'Season of Passage', a sanctioned culling dressed as ceremony — and frees a penned group; the sheer volume of unsanctioned magic finally draws a WHITE CAPE INVESTIGATOR — Aldric Vane — out of the treeline the moment the captors fall. He does not attack: he formally declares Caelen UNDER ARREST for the practice of magic. It is that sentence that breaks the freed elves — seeing in Caelen the first hope their people have had, and knowing they cannot defeat a White Cape, they arm themselves and charge him anyway, spending their lives to buy Caelen the time to escape west. The White Cape uses the Mage stat block and is far beyond Caelen at this level: the chapter is an escape paid for in his kin's blood, not a boss fight. (CH3 Into Hiding) Caelen crosses a dead waystation at dawn toward the brazier-lit warren arch while Vane hunts him BY THE TRACE OF MAGIC — every cast pulls the hunter a stage closer, a quiet crossing never alerts him; the second surviving elf lies pinned under fallen stone, and freeing her (the chapter-one Help gesture) is loud. The adventure ENDS at the arch with Caelen's revenge oath — hardened into purpose if his kin came through with him, weighted with a first debt of guilt if one was left for Vane's ledger.

Key NPCs: the captive elves (frightened, grateful, quietly afraid of what Caelen's power cost him); Investigator Aldric Vane, the White Cape (cold, bureaucratic, lethal — he catalogues unsanctioned casters and then collars or kills them, and speaks of killings as ledger entries; never let the player win a straight fight with him). Theme: oppression answered with a deal that may be worse than the oppression. Caelen is sympathetic and damned in equal measure.
